reviewer: retrospective critique backfill (code-grounded + existing conformance suite), revision 1

rubric.evidence: worker.js loads a REAL transformers.js zero-shot-classification pipeline (loadPipeline({task:'zero-shot-classification', model:'Xenova/bart-large-mnli', backend:'wasm', dtype:'q4f16'})) — THE canonical zero-shot classifier (facebook/bart-large fine-tuned on MultiNLI). It calls pipe(text, labels, {hypothesis_template:'This example is {}.', multi_label}) on-device and reuses the model instance to expose raw 3-way NLI logits. Conformance calls-real-inference (source) + loads-clean (console-clean) tested pass; io-shape-semantic manual-evidenced. No measured latency fabricated.
rubric.notes: IMPORTANT DTYPE HONESTY (from the worker header): this demo ships q4f16, NOT the usual q8. The code documents that the q8 (int8) build of this model is BADLY DEGRADED in-browser — near-uniform scores, frequently the wrong label (e.g. 'the phone overheats' → 'billing problem'). q4f16 (4-bit weights + fp16 compute) restores full discrimination (→ 'hardware fault') at a SMALLER download than q8 and still runs on WASM. This is a measured, disclosed choice of the smallest dtype that genuinely works — a strong example of honest engineering, and models.json correctly records dtype q4f16 (matched by the declares-quantisation assertion).

rubric.evidence: Expected output = labels ranked by entailment score. worker.js returns scored[] from the pipeline plus nli[]: raw [contradiction,neutral,entailment] logits + softmax per hypothesis (classNamesOf reads pipe.model.config.id2label). It also has a contrast mode that runs the SAME text+labels through DeBERTa-v3-xsmall for a side-by-side, and a classifyWith backbone selector for the multi-model cascade. io-shape-semantic conformance assertion covers real ranked labels.
rubric.notes: multi_label toggles softmax-over-labels vs independent per-label entailment — real pipeline semantics.

rubric.evidence: At-a-glance declares Xenova/bart-large-mnli (linked, 5 occurrences), the zero-shot-classification task, q4f16 dtype, WASM backend, ~309 MB and MIT license — matching models.json. declares-model-id/task/backend/quantisation conformance assertions tested pass (quant test string q4f16).
rubric.notes: The contrast model Xenova/nli-deberta-v3-xsmall (~70 MB) is loaded lazily only when the user asks for a comparison — disclosed. Inference off the main thread.

rubric.evidence: The page uses the shared createModelLoader auto-init (modelId Xenova/bart-large-mnli, q4f16, ~309 MB, requiresWebGPU:false); a valid current cached copy auto-initialises with no Load click. The contrast DeBERTa is loaded on demand via a separate loadCompare path. shared-auto-init-loader conformance assertion tested pass.
rubric.notes: ~309 MB primary + optional ~70 MB contrast — the never-silently-re-download guarantee + Clear-cache matter; sizes surfaced in at-a-glance.

rubric.evidence: runStatus + a second contrast status are role=status aria-live (2 role=status regions, 5 aria-live regions on the page); worker errors surface on the page; shared loader adds checking→initialising→ready + Retry; the contrast path has its own cmpProgress/cmpReady messages. live-status-region + errors-on-the-page conformance assertions tested pass; honest-progress-retry-offline / runs-without-webgpu-fallback manual-evidenced.
rubric.notes: WASM-only. The separate progress channel for the contrast model keeps its download state honest and distinct from the primary model.

rubric.evidence: Real, labelled controls: the text <textarea>, the candidate-labels input, a multi-label toggle, a Run button, and a contrast control that runs both backbones. All keyboard-operable. controls-operable conformance assertion is manual-evidenced.
rubric.notes: The contrast mode is a genuine model-vs-model interpretability control, not decorative — it loads the second backbone and shows both rankings + timings.

rubric.evidence: No canned scores: ranked labels, the 3-way NLI bars and the contrast rankings/timings are all from the real pipeline(s). no-fake-output conformance assertion is manual-evidenced.
rubric.notes: The dtype choice itself is an anti-fake measure: shipping q4f16 instead of the broken q8 ensures the visible scores are actually discriminative rather than near-uniform noise.

rubric.evidence: Run + contrast buttons carry names (buttons-have-names tested pass); both status regions are role=status aria-live; result/contrast regions aria-live; page lang=en; WCAG-AA light+dark. images-have-alt tested pass.
rubric.notes: Score bars and NLI bars are DOM with text percentages, inspectable by assistive tech.

rubric.evidence: responsive-desktop/responsive-mobile (no-horizontal-overflow) conformance assertions present; design-system panels + flex-wrap bars. models.json support is needs-review on both classes (automated pre-scan flagged sub-24px tap targets — 12 desktop / 6 mobile), pending an agent matrix pass to confirm tap sizes and flip to ok.
rubric.notes: This page has the most flagged sub-24px targets in the batch (the label chips + contrast controls); recorded honestly as needs-review.

rubric.evidence: All inference (primary, per-label NLI passes, and the contrast backbone) runs in the Web Worker; the main thread only renders bars. inference-off-main-thread conformance assertion tested pass; inp-stays-low manual-evidenced.
rubric.notes: BART-large is the heaviest of the two backbones; each label is a separate forward pass, so many labels + contrast mode is compute-heavy — but off-thread, so INP is protected. Perceived latency (not INP) is the cost here.

guidanceConsulted.query: keep INP low move heavy work off main thread web worker
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Run heavy inference off the main thread; keep INP low even during compute-heavy runs.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — primary BART, per-label NLI passes and the contrast DeBERTa all run in the worker; the main thread only paints scores.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: worker.js off-thread; inference-off-main-thread conformance assertion tested pass

guidanceConsulted.query: accessible form labels text input textarea
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Label every input; keyboard-operable; invent-your-own labels as a plain text field.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — text, candidate-labels, multi-label and contrast controls are labelled and keyboard-operable.

guidanceConsulted.query: accessible live region status role status aria-live
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Announce state via role=status aria-live; use distinct regions for distinct async work.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — primary and contrast each have their own role=status aria-live region; Run/contrast buttons named.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: live-status-region (2 role=status regions) + buttons-have-names conformance assertions tested pass

guidanceConsulted.query: responsive control panel without horizontal overflow flex grid
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Fluid flex/grid with wrapping; avoid fixed widths.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — design-system panels; score/NLI/contrast bars flex-wrap.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: responsive assertions present; support needs-review (12/6 sub-24px flags) pending agent matrix pass

openQuestions: This page carries the most flagged sub-24px tap targets in the batch (12 desktop / 6 mobile) — which specific chips/controls need a min-height bump to clear the ≈44px guideline?
Should the page state its q4f16-over-q8 rationale in the UI (it's currently in the worker header), so users understand why this demo's dtype differs from the sibling text demos?

followUpGoals.goal: Surface the q4f16-vs-q8 rationale in the page UI (the q8 build is measurably degraded), so the honest dtype choice is visible to users, not only in code.
Run the mobile+desktop responsive matrix, raise the flagged sub-24px tap targets (12 desktop / 6 mobile), and flip models.json support to ok.

summary: Retrospective, code-grounded revision-1 critique for bart-zero-shot (Xenova/bart-large-mnli, zero-shot-classification, q4f16, ~309 MB, MIT — THE canonical NLI zero-shot classifier). worker.js runs the transformers.js pipeline off the main thread, exposes raw 3-way NLI logits, and offers a genuine contrast mode running the SAME text+labels through DeBERTa-v3-xsmall side-by-side. STANDOUT honesty: the demo ships q4f16, NOT q8, because the code measured the q8 build to be badly degraded (near-uniform, wrong labels) — q4f16 restores discrimination at a smaller download; models.json + the declares-quantisation assertion correctly record q4f16. Auto-init via createModelLoader; two distinct role=status aria-live regions; no fake output. Verified against the immutable conformance suite (calls-real-inference, loads-clean, declares-*, inference-off-main-thread, shared-auto-init-loader, live-status-region, buttons-have-names tested pass; io-shape/parity/inp manual-evidenced). No fabricated numbers. Honest weaknesses: most sub-24px tap-target flags in the batch (12 desktop / 6 mobile), support needs-review; the q4f16 rationale lives only in code; cost scales with label count + contrast mode (off-thread, so perceived latency not INP).
